knowledge.html:
IX. REPORTED SPEECH (TƯỜNG THUẬT)

1. Cách thay đổi trong câu tường thuật

Lùi thì (Lùi xuống 1 thì)

Cấu trúc tiếpCâu tường thuật
1. Hiện tại đơn
V/Vs/Ves/am/is/are/don't+V1/doesn't+V1		=> Quá khứ đơn
V2/Ved/was/were/didn't+V
2. Hiện tại tiếp diễn
am/is/are + V-ing		=> Quá khứ tiếp diễn
was/were + V-ing
3. Hiện tại hoàn thành
have/has + V3/ed		=> Quá khứ hoàn thành
had + V3/ed
4. Quá khứ đơn
was/were/V2/Ved		=> Quá khứ hoàn thành
had + V3/ed
5. Quá khứ tiếp diễn
was/were + V-ing		=> Quá khứ hoàn thành tiếp diễn
had + been + V-ing
6. Tương lai đơn
will + V		=> Tương lai trong quá khứ
would + V
7. can=> could
8. may=> might
9. must/need=> had to

Thì QKHT, ought to, could, should, might, used to, would rather, had better... => giữ nguyên

2. Đổi ngôi

- Đổi ngôi thứ nhất (I, we, me, us, my, our, mine, ours) phù hợp với Chủ ngữ trong mệnh đề chính.
- Đổi ngôi thứ hai (you, your, your) phù hợp với Tân ngữ trong mệnh đề chính.
- Ngôi thứ BA (he, she, it, him, her, his, they, them, their) => không đổi.

Trực tiếpTường thuậtTrực tiếpTường thuật
IHe (nam)/She (nữ)Wethey
mehim/herusthem
myhis/herourtheir
minehis/hersourstheirs
myselfhimself/herselfourselvesthemselves

3. Đổi trạng từ

Trực tiếpTường thuật
thisthat
thesethose
nowthen
herethere
todaythat day
tonightthat night
agobefore
yesterdaythe previous day / the day before
tomorrowthe following day / the day after
last...the previous...
next...the following

Lưu ý: không dùng dấu ngoặc kép, dấu chấm hỏi, dấu chấm than, dấu hai chấm trong lời nói tường thuật.

Các trường hợp không đổi thì:

  1. Khi động từ tường thuật ở thì hiện tại hoặc tương lai (say, tell, have told, will say...)
  2. Thì quá khứ đơn có thời gian xác định.
  3. Diễn tả 1 chân lý, sự thật hiển nhiên.
  4. Câu điều kiện loại 2 và 3.

4. Các mẫu câu tường thuật cần nắm rõ

Statements (Câu trần thuật)

S + say / say to SO / tell SO + (that) + clause
Ex: Tom said, "I am very busy."
=> Tom said (that) he was very busy.

Commands/Requests (Mệnh lệnh/Yêu cầu)

Khẳng định: S + told/asked/advised... + O + to V1
Phủ định: S + told/asked/advised... + O + not + to V1
Ex: "Hurry up, Lan!" => He told Lan to hurry up.
Ex: "Don't leave this room." => He ordered them not to leave that room.

Yes-No Questions

S + asked (O) / wanted to know + if/whether + S + V (lùi thì)
Ex: He said, "Can you speak English?"
=> He asked me if/whether I could speak English.

Wh- Questions

S + asked (O) / wanted to know + what/when/why + S + V (lùi thì)
Ex: He said, "When did you leave the room?"
=> He wanted to know when I had left the room.

Loại yêu cầu

Would/Could/Will/Can you + V => S + asked/told + O + to V
Would you mind/Do you mind + V-ing => S + asked/told + O + to V

Tường thuật với to V

Tiếng ViệtĐộng từCấu trúc
Yêu cầuasked+ O + to V
Mờiinvited+ O + to V
Khuyênadvised+ O + to V
Nhắc nhởreminded+ O + to V
Ra lệnhordered+ O + to V
Khuyến khíchencouraged+ O + to V
Cảnh báowarned+ O + to V
Muốnwanted+ O + to V
Đồng ýagreed+ to V
Hứapromised+ to V

Tường thuật với V-ing

Tiếng ViệtĐộng từCấu trúc
Cảm ơnthanked+ O + for + V-ing
Xin lỗiapologized(to O) + for + V-ing
Buộc tộiaccused+ O + of + V-ing
Chúc mừngcongratulated+ O + on + V-ing
Cảnh báowarned+ O + against + V-ing
Ngăn ngừastopped/prevented+ O + from + V-ing
Đề nghịsuggested+ V-ing / suggested that + S + (should) + V
Khăng khănginsisted+ on/upon + V-ing
Phủ nhậndenied+ V-ing
Thừa nhậnadmitted+ V-ing / having V3/ed

exercises.question: Nam wanted to know what time _______.
exercises.explanation: Reported Wh-question: use statement word order (no inversion) and backshift tense. 'What time does the movie begin?' becomes 'what time the movie began'.

exercises.question: I wondered _______ the right thing.
exercises.explanation: Reported yes/no questions use if/whether + statement order + backshifted tense: 'whether I was doing'.

exercises.question: He told me _______ him up at six o'clock.
exercises.explanation: Reported commands/requests: told + object + to V. So 'Pick me up' becomes 'told me to pick him up'.

exercises.question: The woman asked _______ catch a bus to school.
exercises.explanation: Reported yes/no question: asked + whether/if + statement order + backshifted tense. 'Can' becomes 'could'.

exercises.question: Bob said he _______ a lot of good marks that semester.
exercises.explanation: Reported statement: backshift present simple to past simple. 'I get' becomes 'he got'.

exercises.question: They told their manager that they _______ their best to do the tasks.
exercises.explanation: In reported speech, 'will' backshifts to 'would': 'will try' becomes 'would try'.

exercises.question: She asked me where I _______ the previous night.
exercises.explanation: Past simple backshifts to past perfect: 'was' becomes 'had been'. Time marker 'last night' changes to 'the previous night'.

exercises.question: She _______ me whether I liked French fries.
exercises.explanation: The reporting verb must match the tense context. Since 'liked' is backshifted, the reporting verb is also past: 'asked'.

exercises.question: "_______ off the television", he said.
exercises.explanation: This is direct speech (a polite command): 'Please turn off the television'.

exercises.question: William said that the train _______ yet.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect negative backshifts to past perfect negative: 'hasn't arrived' becomes 'hadn't arrived'.

exercises.question: He asked the children _______ the electric sockets.
exercises.explanation: Reported negative command: asked + object + not to V. 'Don't touch' becomes 'not to touch'.

exercises.question: David asked me who _______ the writer of that book.
exercises.explanation: Reported Wh-question with backshifting: 'is' becomes 'was'. Statement word order is used.

exercises.question: He wants to know whether I _______ back to Hoi An the next day.
exercises.explanation: Although 'wants' is present tense, the time marker 'the next day' (changed from 'tomorrow') indicates the reported content has been backshifted. So 'will come' becomes 'would come'. This is a common exam pattern testing consistency of backshifting within the reported clause.

exercises.question: They asked me how many students _______.
exercises.explanation: Reported Wh-question: statement word order + backshifted tense. 'How many students do you teach?' becomes 'how many students I taught'.

exercises.question: Laura wanted to know _______ shopping the previous afternoon.
options: A. if we had been going
B. that if we had been going
exercises.explanation: Reported yes/no question: wanted to know + if + statement order + backshifted tense. Past continuous 'were going' backshifts to past perfect continuous 'had been going'.

exercises.question: Lucy asked me why I had not gone to Paris _______.
exercises.explanation: In reported speech, 'last summer' changes to 'the summer before' (or 'the previous summer').

exercises.question: Tony told me that they _______ meat two days _______.
exercises.explanation: Present perfect backshifts to past perfect: 'have not eaten' becomes 'had not eaten'. Time marker 'ago' changes to 'before'.

exercises.question: Charlie _______ that his father was in hospital.
exercises.explanation: Per Murphy: 'told' takes a direct object (told me), never 'told to me'. 'Said' cannot take a person object directly ('said me' is wrong). So the correct form is 'told me'.

exercises.question: Julia said that she _______ there at noon.
exercises.explanation: Lùi thì: is going to => was going to.

exercises.question: He said that Linda and John _______ married _______.
options: B. are getting / the next day
C. were getting / the next day
D. will getting / the day after
exercises.explanation: Lùi thì: are getting => were getting. 'tomorrow' => 'the next day'.

exercises.original: "Peter, do you prefer tea or coffee?" she said.
exercises.answer: She asked Peter if he preferred tea or coffee.
acceptedAnswers: She asked Peter if he preferred tea or coffee.
She asked Peter if he preferred tea or coffee
She asked Peter whether he preferred tea or coffee.
She asked Peter whether he preferred tea or coffee
exercises.explanation: Yes-No question tường thuật: asked + O + if/whether + S + V (lùi thì).

exercises.original: "The film began at seven o'clock," he said.
exercises.answer: He said that the film had begun at seven o'clock.
acceptedAnswers: He said that the film had begun at seven o'clock.
He said that the film had begun at seven o'clock
He said the film had begun at seven o'clock.
He said the film had begun at seven o'clock
exercises.explanation: Trần thuật: said + (that) + S + V (lùi thì). began => had begun.

exercises.original: The father said, "I was on a business trip the whole day yesterday."
exercises.answer: The father said that he had been on a business trip the whole day the day before.
acceptedAnswers: The father said that he had been on a business trip the whole day the day before.
The father said that he had been on a business trip the whole day the day before
The father said he had been on a business trip the whole day the day before.
The father said he had been on a business trip the whole day the day before
The father said that he had been on a business trip the whole day the previous day.
The father said that he had been on a business trip the whole day the previous day
exercises.explanation: was => had been. 'yesterday' => 'the day before' hoặc 'the previous day'. Lùi thì.

exercises.original: "My brother will get married next month," James said.
exercises.answer: James said that his brother would get married the following month.
acceptedAnswers: James said that his brother would get married the following month.
James said that his brother would get married the following month
James said his brother would get married the following month.
James said his brother would get married the following month
James said that his brother would get married the next month.
James said that his brother would get married the next month
exercises.explanation: will => would. 'next month' => 'the following month' hoặc 'the next month'. 'my' => 'his'. Lùi thì.

exercises.original: Henry said to his mother, "Come and spend a week with us."
exercises.answer: Henry told his mother to come and spend a week with them.
acceptedAnswers: Henry told his mother to come and spend a week with them.
Henry told his mother to come and spend a week with them
exercises.explanation: Mệnh lệnh: told + O + to V. 'us' => 'them'.

exercises.original: He said to me, "Keep a seat for me in the lecture hall."
exercises.answer: He told me to keep a seat for him in the lecture hall.
acceptedAnswers: He told me to keep a seat for him in the lecture hall.
He told me to keep a seat for him in the lecture hall
He asked me to keep a seat for him in the lecture hall.
He asked me to keep a seat for him in the lecture hall
exercises.explanation: Mệnh lệnh: told/asked + O + to V. 'me' (trong lời nói) => 'him'.

exercises.original: Alfred said to John, "I did not promise to send you a telegram."
exercises.answer: Alfred told John that he had not promised to send him a telegram.
acceptedAnswers: Alfred told John that he had not promised to send him a telegram.
Alfred told John that he had not promised to send him a telegram
Alfred told John he had not promised to send him a telegram.
Alfred told John he had not promised to send him a telegram
Alfred told John that he hadn't promised to send him a telegram.
Alfred told John that he hadn't promised to send him a telegram
exercises.explanation: did not promise => had not promised. 'I' => 'he'. 'you' => 'him'.

exercises.original: "I will get myself a drink," she said.
exercises.answer: She said that she would get herself a drink.
acceptedAnswers: She said that she would get herself a drink.
She said that she would get herself a drink
She said she would get herself a drink.
She said she would get herself a drink
exercises.explanation: will => would. 'I' => 'she'. 'myself' => 'herself'.

exercises.original: "I cannot drive them home," he said.
exercises.answer: He said that he could not drive them home.
acceptedAnswers: He said that he could not drive them home.
He said that he could not drive them home
He said he could not drive them home.
He said he could not drive them home
He said that he couldn't drive them home.
He said that he couldn't drive them home
exercises.explanation: cannot => could not. 'I' => 'he'.

exercises.original: Mike said to Henry, "Give me my book back, please."
exercises.answer: Mike asked Henry to give him his book back.
acceptedAnswers: Mike asked Henry to give him his book back.
Mike asked Henry to give him his book back
Mike told Henry to give him his book back.
Mike told Henry to give him his book back
exercises.explanation: Yêu cầu lịch sự (có 'please'): asked + O + to V. 'me' => 'him'. 'my' => 'his'.

exercises.wrongSentence: He said that he will pick me up at 8 am the following day.
exercises.correctSentence: He said that he would pick me up at 8 am the following day.
exercises.explanation: Trong tường thuật, 'will' phải lùi thành 'would'.

exercises.wrongSentence: She said that the books in the library would be available tomorrow.
exercises.correctSentence: She said that the books in the library would be available the next day.
exercises.explanation: 'tomorrow' phải đổi thành 'the next day / the following day' trong tường thuật.

exercises.wrongSentence: Henry said me to come and spend a week with him.
exercises.correctSentence: Henry told me to come and spend a week with him.
exercises.explanation: 'say' không dùng với tân ngữ trực tiếp. Phải dùng 'told me to...'.

exercises.wrongSentence: The receptionist said I must fill in that form before I attended the interview.
exercises.correctSentence: The receptionist said I had to fill in that form before I attended the interview.
exercises.explanation: 'must' lùi thành 'had to' trong tường thuật.

exercises.wrongSentence: When I was a teenager, my dad always told me that I hadn't used to stay out later than ten o'clock.
exercises.correctSentence: When I was a teenager, my dad always told me not to stay out later than ten o'clock.
exercises.errorWord: that I hadn't used to stay
exercises.explanation: Đây là lời khuyên/mệnh lệnh tường thuật: told + O + not to V. 'Hadn't used to' không phù hợp vì ý nghĩa là 'báo không được đi chơi khuya', không phải 'không quen đi chơi khuya'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I asked him how far was it from my school to the post office if I went there by taxi.
exercises.correctSentence: I asked him how far it was from my school to the post office if I went there by taxi.
exercises.explanation: Trong câu tường thuật, dùng trật tự câu trần thuật (S + V), không đảo ngữ.

exercises.question: Marty said a good friendship _______ like a diamond: valuable, beautiful and durable.
exercises.explanation: Đây là chân lý, sự thật hiển nhiên (a good friendship is like a diamond) nên không lùi thì trong tường thuật. Giữ nguyên 'is'.

exercises.wrongSentence: I asked him how far was it to the station if I went there by taxi.
exercises.correctSentence: I asked him how far it was to the station if I went there by taxi.
exercises.explanation: Tường thuật Wh-question dùng trật tự trần thuật: S + V, không đảo ngữ.

exercises.question: All of my students wondered how many people lived in Tokyo? (Choose the underlined part that needs correcting: A. All of, B. wondered, C. many, D. ?)
exercises.explanation: Câu tường thuật không dùng dấu chấm hỏi. Phải thay '?' bằng '.'.

exercises.question: They asked me that I could do the shopping for them. (Choose the underlined part that needs correcting: A. asked, B. that, C. could do, D. for)
exercises.explanation: Tường thuật Yes-No question dùng 'if/whether', không dùng 'that'. 'that' => 'if'.
